variants.text: Between cases again. You've gone deeper into the stacks than anyone since Day Zero — deeper than the records say is good for a person. The board's still got work on it. It always does. ...Take a breath first. You've earned the second between cases.

...You've started keeping your own ledger. I won't report it. Filing's my whole job, friend — I know a filing hand when I see one. Look closer at who logs your cases. And don't leave that ledger on the desk. Things filed here get interpreted.
Back at the board. Good — keep moving while the cases are still legible. They get stranger the deeper you go. You'll see what I mean. You're already seeing it.
The case board's over there, on the far wall. Pick what's active. The locked ones open when you've earned them. That's how it works — says so in my own handbook, which I wrote, apparently.

choices.text: How does the board decide what's active?

board.speaker: Vasic
board.text: Each case unlocks the next. Officially it's 'clearance progression.' Unofficially — since Day Zero the Archive only shows people what it thinks they can survive knowing. Finish one and it decides you're ready for the next layer down. The board isn't a list. It's a leash with slack let out slowly.

rest.speaker: Vasic
rest.text: Rest while you can. The Bureau's the only place that stays still. Every case takes you somewhere that doesn't. This room, this board, me complaining at you — for most of us it's the only home we can be *sure* of, because it's the only one we've had since the world forgot itself. Funny that it's an office.

bye.speaker: Vasic
bye.text: Go on. The board's waiting. It's always waiting. So am I, apparently.
